suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker used the web-feature-worker skill for a schema-only Supabase migration feature. The skill procedure is heavily UI/browser oriented and does not mention Supabase migration/advisor workflows, even though this milestone requires live database schema work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: features.json assigns skillName web-feature-worker to leagueback-player-sync-metadata; transcript skeleton shows the worker following that skill while primarily using supabase migration tools.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker feedback shows a recurring startup pitfall on Windows: worker-base still lets sessions attempt .factory/init.sh in ways that are not appropriate for this host, even though mission guidance says to treat it as optional/no-op without a POSIX shell.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session 9a2caa52-cea5-4c64-beac-541a7ca62514 includes python3 C:\Users\bendc\Documents\GitHub\Leagueback-WEBAPP\.factory\init.sh, and handoff.skillFeedback explicitly asks for clearer worker-base guidance for Windows.

rejectedObservations.observation: The mission library now names `Bumsdito#3005` as the preferred returning-player validation account, but it still does not spell out the minimum evidence needed to prove this feature: re-read the DB-backed route after reconciliation and confirm the formerly missing recent IDs are persisted there without duplication.
The mission library did not capture that stale derived detection must be match-specific rather than only player-wide.
